# youtrack_global_time_tracking_settings (Resource)

YouTrack global time tracking settings resource. This resource manages the global work time schedule and exposes related global work item type and attribute data.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Configure global time tracking settings with a standard 8-hour work day (Mon–Fri)
# and explicitly manage the list of work item types.
resource "youtrack_global_time_tracking_settings" "global" {
  work_time_settings = {
    # 8 hours × 60 minutes = 480 minutes per working day
    minutes_a_day = 480
    # 1 = Monday, 2 = Tuesday, 3 = Wednesday, 4 = Thursday, 5 = Friday
    # 0 = Sunday, 6 = Saturday
    work_days = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
  }

  # When set, this list is the source of truth for work item types:
  # types declared here are created/updated, types absent here are deleted.
  # Omit this block entirely to leave work item types unmanaged (read-only).
  work_item_types = [
    { name = "Development", auto_attached = true },
    { name = "Testing", auto_attached = true },
    { name = "Documentation", auto_attached = false },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `work_time_settings` (Attributes) System-wide work schedule settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--work_time_settings))

### Optional

- `work_item_types` (Attributes Set) Set of work item types to manage. When set, this set is the source of truth: types present here are created or updated in YouTrack, types absent here are deleted. When omitted, work item types are read from the API and stored as computed state. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--work_item_types))

### Read-Only

- `attribute_prototypes` (Attributes List) Read-only list of global work item attribute prototypes and their values. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--attribute_prototypes))
- `id` (String) The ID of the global time tracking settings configuration.
- `last_updated` (String) Timestamp of the last update

<a id="nestedatt--work_time_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `work_time_settings`

Required:

- `minutes_a_day` (Number) Number of minutes in a working day.
- `work_days` (List of Number) Indexes of working days in week. Sunday is 0, Monday is 1, and so on.

Read-Only:

- `days_a_week` (Number) Number of working days a week reported by the server.
- `first_day_of_week` (Number) Index of the first day of week reported by the server.
- `id` (String) Work time settings identifier.


<a id="nestedatt--work_item_types"></a>
### Nested Schema for `work_item_types`

Optional:

- `auto_attached` (Boolean) When true, this type is automatically added to a project when time tracking is enabled.
- `name` (String) Name of the work item type.


<a id="nestedatt--attribute_prototypes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `attribute_prototypes`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String)
- `instances` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--attribute_prototypes--instances))
- `name` (String)
- `values` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--attribute_prototypes--values))

<a id="nestedatt--attribute_prototypes--instances"></a>
### Nested Schema for `attribute_prototypes.instances`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String)
- `name` (String)
- `ordinal` (Number)
- `values` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--attribute_prototypes--instances--values))

<a id="nestedatt--attribute_prototypes--instances--values"></a>
### Nested Schema for `attribute_prototypes.instances.values`

Read-Only:

- `auto_attach` (Boolean)
- `description` (String)
- `id` (String)
- `name` (String)



<a id="nestedatt--attribute_prototypes--values"></a>
### Nested Schema for `attribute_prototypes.values`

Read-Only:

- `auto_attach` (Boolean)
- `description` (String)
- `id` (String)
- `name` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Global time tracking settings is a singleton resource.
# You can use any value for the import ID (it will be ignored).
# The resource will fetch the current global time tracking settings.
terraform import youtrack_global_time_tracking_settings.global global-time-tracking
```
